title: Pyrus subarranensis
taxonomy: Pyrus subarranensis belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Amygdaloideae and Maloideae, to which Pyrus subarranensis is assigned. The genus Pyrus, to which Pyrus subarranensis belongs, comprises species of pear trees and their allies. Pyrus subarranensis is classified as a species within the genus Pyrus. The specific classification of Pyrus subarranensis includes its taxonomic hierarchy as Pyrus subarranensis, a species within the family Rosaceae.
uses: Pyrus subarranensis is a species of pear tree native to South America, but information on its specific medicinal uses is limited. intro: Pyrus subpalmata, also known as Pyrus subarranensis, is a species of pear tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus subarranensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pyrus subarranensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and fever, as well as to promote digestive health and relaxation.
